categories.title: Software Engineering
categories.description: Strategies for designing, delivering, operating, and documenting software systems.

categories.title: Mobile Engineering
categories.description: Strategies for designing, delivering, operating, and releasing mobile applications.

capabilities.title: Software Delivery
capabilities.description: Deliver maintainable software changes with explicit validation.

capabilities.title: Mobile Delivery
capabilities.description: Deliver mobile changes with device and release validation.

deliverables.title: Software Change
deliverables.description: A reviewed and validated software change.

deliverables.title: Mobile Release
deliverables.description: A reviewed and validated mobile release.

strategies.title: Modular Monolith
strategies.description: Organize one deployable system around explicit internal module boundaries.

evidence.summary: Use when one deployment unit benefits from explicit ownership boundaries.

strategies.title: Clean Architecture
strategies.description: Separate domain rules from delivery and infrastructure concerns.

evidence.summary: Use when dependency direction and domain isolation are explicit project goals.

strategies.title: Hexagonal Architecture
strategies.description: Place external integrations behind ports and adapters.

evidence.summary: Use when integration substitution and boundary testing justify adapter overhead.

strategies.title: Vertical Slice Delivery
strategies.description: Deliver behavior in end-to-end slices with focused ownership.

evidence.summary: Use when work can be organized around independently reviewable behavior.

strategies.title: Trunk-Based Development
strategies.description: Integrate small changes frequently into a protected main branch.

evidence.summary: Use with reliable automated checks and small reversible changes.

strategies.title: Feature Flag Delivery
strategies.description: Separate deployment from release using owned and removable flags.

evidence.summary: Use when rollout control justifies flag lifecycle management.

strategies.title: API Contract First
strategies.description: Review interface contracts before implementation and integration.

evidence.summary: Use when multiple consumers require coordinated interface evolution.

strategies.title: REST Resource Modeling
strategies.description: Model HTTP interfaces around resources, representations, and status semantics.

evidence.summary: Use for HTTP APIs where resource semantics fit the domain.

strategies.title: Event-Driven Integration
strategies.description: Integrate components through versioned events and explicit delivery semantics.

evidence.summary: Use when asynchronous decoupling justifies operational complexity.

strategies.title: Relational Data Modeling
strategies.description: Model durable data with explicit keys, constraints, and ownership.

evidence.summary: Use when relational integrity matches the data and query model.

strategies.title: Safe Schema Migrations
strategies.description: Plan backward-compatible data changes with verification and rollback.

evidence.summary: Use for production data changes that must preserve live compatibility.

strategies.title: Unit Testing
strategies.description: Validate focused behavior at stable module boundaries.

evidence.summary: Use where focused deterministic tests provide useful feedback.

strategies.title: Integration Testing
strategies.description: Validate interactions across real component boundaries.

evidence.summary: Use for critical boundaries not sufficiently covered by isolated tests.

strategies.title: Contract Testing
strategies.description: Validate provider and consumer expectations for shared interfaces.

evidence.summary: Use when independently delivered consumers and providers share contracts.

strategies.title: Threat Modeling
strategies.description: Identify assets, trust boundaries, threats, and mitigations before release.

evidence.summary: Use for changes that introduce sensitive data or trust-boundary effects.

strategies.title: Least Privilege
strategies.description: Grant identities and components only the access required for their task.

evidence.summary: Use when defining application, service, or deployment permissions.

strategies.title: Progressive Delivery
strategies.description: Release changes incrementally with monitored rollback criteria.

evidence.summary: Use when production rollout risk benefits from staged exposure.

strategies.title: Infrastructure as Code
strategies.description: Review and reproduce infrastructure through versioned declarative definitions.

evidence.summary: Use when infrastructure changes need reviewability and reproducibility.

strategies.title: Observability and SLOs
strategies.description: Define service indicators, objectives, and actionable telemetry.

evidence.summary: Use for operated services requiring explicit reliability signals.

strategies.title: Architecture Decision Records
strategies.description: Record consequential technical decisions, context, and tradeoffs.

evidence.summary: Use for decisions future contributors need to understand or revisit.

strategies.title: Native iOS
strategies.description: Build an iOS application using platform-native APIs and release tooling.

evidence.summary: Select when iOS-specific capability and platform integration are primary.

strategies.title: Native Android
strategies.description: Build an Android application using platform-native APIs and release tooling.

evidence.summary: Select when Android-specific capability and platform integration are primary.

strategies.title: React Native
strategies.description: Share mobile application code while preserving reviewed native integration boundaries.

evidence.summary: Select when shared JavaScript or TypeScript delivery matches team and product constraints.

strategies.title: Flutter
strategies.description: Share mobile UI and application code through a Flutter delivery model.

evidence.summary: Select when a shared Dart and Flutter stack matches team and product constraints.

strategies.title: Mobile Accessibility
strategies.description: Review semantics, focus, text scaling, contrast, and assistive-technology behavior.

evidence.summary: Apply to every user-facing mobile delivery.

strategies.title: Mobile Offline First
strategies.description: Design core mobile tasks to remain useful under intermittent connectivity.

evidence.summary: Use when network availability cannot be assumed for core tasks.

strategies.title: Mobile Sync Conflict Resolution
strategies.description: Define deterministic merge and user-recovery behavior for synchronized mobile data.

evidence.summary: Use when offline or concurrent changes can produce data conflicts.

strategies.title: App Store Release
strategies.description: Plan signing, metadata, privacy declarations, review, and staged store release.

evidence.summary: Apply when distributing through a mobile application store.

strategies.title: Mobile Unit Testing
strategies.description: Validate deterministic mobile domain and presentation behavior in isolation.

evidence.summary: Use for mobile behavior that can be tested without devices or emulators.

strategies.title: Mobile UI Testing
strategies.description: Validate critical mobile journeys on representative devices or emulators.

evidence.summary: Use for critical journeys where platform behavior matters.

strategies.title: Deep Linking
strategies.description: Define validated routing, fallback, and authorization behavior for incoming links.

evidence.summary: Use when external links open specific application destinations.

strategies.title: Push Notifications
strategies.description: Design permission, delivery, routing, and user-control behavior for notifications.

evidence.summary: Use only when notifications provide user-controlled product value.

strategies.title: Mobile Secure Storage
strategies.description: Store sensitive mobile data using platform-provided protected storage and explicit lifecycles.

evidence.summary: Use when mobile devices must retain sensitive application data.

strategies.title: Mobile Performance Budgets
strategies.description: Set and validate startup, interaction, memory, and rendering budgets.

evidence.summary: Use where device variability makes performance an explicit acceptance concern.

strategies.title: Battery and Network Budgets
strategies.description: Bound background work, radio use, and data transfer for mobile tasks.

evidence.summary: Use for background or data-intensive mobile behavior.

strategies.title: Responsive Mobile Layout
strategies.description: Design for supported screen sizes, orientations, and input modes.

evidence.summary: Apply where mobile layouts must adapt across supported form factors.

strategies.title: Mobile Localization
strategies.description: Design strings, layouts, formats, and tests for supported locales.

evidence.summary: Use when mobile releases support more than one locale.

strategies.title: Crash Reporting
strategies.description: Collect privacy-reviewed crash evidence and connect it to release decisions.

evidence.summary: Use to diagnose release stability while respecting privacy constraints.

strategies.title: Mobile Phased Rollout
strategies.description: Release mobile versions gradually with monitored stop criteria.

evidence.summary: Use when store tooling and release risk justify staged availability.

strategies.title: Mobile Analytics Privacy
strategies.description: Define minimal analytics events, consent, retention, and user controls.

evidence.summary: Use when product measurement is required and privacy constraints are explicit.
